summary: A possession-with-control 4-2-3-1 / 4-3-3 that attacks wide and is great in transition from a low-block — but needs a stricter defensive structure. James' return could echo Brazil 2014.
style: 4-2-3-1 / 4-3-3 — play around to attack wide, crosses into the box (#1 in South America), possession with control, great in transition from a low-block.

counterReliance.note: Possession-led but great in transition from a low-block

strengths: #1 in South America for crosses into the box
Excellent in transition from a low-block

keyPlayers.name: Luis Díaz
keyPlayers.note: Goals, assists & creativity — direct dribbling, pace in transition, 45 goal involvements for Bayern

tactics: Work it around to attack wide and cross, keep control of the ball, and break from a low-block. With James Rodríguez back, the recipe could echo their Brazil 2014 run.
notes: The return of James Rodríguez alongside Díaz could be the perfect recipe.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
